rag.description: The InvokeDoubleClick action performs a double-click on a target web element or at the current mouse position when no element is specified. When an element is provided the action moves the mouse to it first to ensure viewport visibility before dispatching the double-click via the WebDriver Actions API.

qa.question: What does the InvokeDoubleClick action do?
qa.answer: InvokeDoubleClick performs a double-click on a target element located by a locator strategy, or double-clicks at the current mouse position when no element is specified.

qa.question: When is the double-click performed at the current mouse position?
qa.answer: When no OnElement is supplied, the action performs a double-click at the last known mouse position without locating any element.

qa.question: Does InvokeDoubleClick move to the element before clicking?
qa.answer: Yes. When an element is provided, the action calls MoveToElement before dispatching the double-click to ensure the element is scrolled into view and interactable.

qa.question: What aliases does InvokeDoubleClick accept?
qa.answer: InvokeDoubleClick is also accepted as DoubleClick and DoubleLeftClick — both resolve to identical behavior.

qa.question: What W3C WebDriver endpoint does InvokeDoubleClick use?
qa.answer: InvokeDoubleClick uses the W3C WebDriver POST /session/{session id}/actions endpoint, dispatching the double-click through the Actions API.

qa.question: What locator strategies does InvokeDoubleClick support?
qa.answer: It supports Xpath (default), CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText via the Locator property. Locator is only evaluated when OnElement is also provided.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for InvokeDoubleClick?
Does InvokeDoubleClick produce any output parameters?
qa.answer: No. InvokeDoubleClick returns a standard plugin response with no additional output parameters.

description: ### Purpose
Performs a double-click on a target element or at the current mouse position when no element is specified.
When an element is provided, the action first moves the mouse cursor to the element to ensure it is scrolled into view before dispatching the double-click via the WebDriver Actions API.
This makes it the primary way to trigger double-click interactions — such as opening items, entering inline edit mode, or activating double-click–driven behaviors — in an automation workflow.
For standard left-click interactions use InvokeClick. For right-click interactions use InvokeContextClick instead.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature             | Description                                                                                      |
| Double Click        | Performs a double-click on the target element using the WebDriver Actions API.                   |
| Auto Scroll         | Calls MoveToElement before clicking to scroll the element into view and ensure interactability.  |
| Positional Click    | Double-clicks at the current mouse position when no element locator is provided.                 |
| Locator Flexibility | Supports Xpath, CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText locator strategies.               |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                | Description                                                                                             |
| File or Item Opening    | Double-click a file, folder, or list item to open it as part of an automated workflow.                  |
| Inline Edit Activation  | Double-click a cell or label to enter inline edit mode in a grid or table.                              |
| Positional Double-Click | Double-click at a scripted mouse position when no stable element reference is available.                |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                | Description                                                                                                 |
| Double-Click Testing    | Verify that double-clickable elements respond correctly to user interaction.                                 |
| Inline Edit Testing     | Confirm that double-clicking a cell or label activates the expected inline edit control.                    |
| Viewport Scroll Testing | Validate that MoveToElement correctly scrolls off-screen elements into view before the double-click occurs. |
| Regression Testing      | Ensure double-click behavior on interactive elements remains consistent after application updates.          |

edge_cases: Element is not in the viewport — MoveToElement scrolls it into view before the double-click
Element is obscured by another element — the double-click may land on the overlapping element instead
Element becomes stale between location and click — a StaleElementReferenceException is raised
Element does not respond to double-click — the browser may suppress the event via JavaScript listeners
Element is inside an iframe — the driver must be switched to the correct frame before locating the element
annotations.expected_result: The element matched by the CSS selector #EditableCell receives a double-click and enters inline edit mode.
annotations.notes: MoveToElement is called before AddDoubleClick to ensure the element is in view and interactable. CssSelector is used because onElement is a CSS selector expression.

description: ### Double-click an element using a CSS selector
Locates the element matching `#EditableCell` using the CssSelector strategy, moves the mouse to it, and performs a double-click.
Use this form for any straightforward double-click interaction on a known stable element, such as activating inline edit mode on a table cell.

edge_cases: No prior mouse movement has occurred — double-click lands at coordinate (0, 0) or the browser default position
Browser focus has shifted to another window — the double-click may not register on the intended document
Mouse position was set by a move action that targeted an element that has since scrolled — position may no longer correspond to the intended target
annotations.expected_result: A double-click is performed at the current mouse cursor position without locating any element.
annotations.notes: When no OnElement is provided the action calls AddDoubleClick().Invoke() on an ActionSequence, dispatching a double-click at the last known mouse position. Useful for scripted mouse-cursor workflows where the position was set by a preceding MoveMouseCursor action.

description: ### Double-click at the current mouse position
Performs a double-click at the last known mouse cursor position without locating or targeting any element.
Use this form when the mouse position has been set by a preceding move action and no element reference is needed.
Locator specifies the strategy used to find the target element for the double-click.
Accepted values include Xpath, CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText.
When absent the default Xpath strategy is used.
Locator is only evaluated when OnElement is also provided; it has no effect in positional-click mode.
properties.name: Locator

description: OnElement provides the locator expression that identifies the element to double-click.
It is evaluated using the strategy defined by the Locator property.
When absent the action performs a double-click at the current mouse position instead.
properties.name: OnElement

summary: InvokeDoubleClick performs a double-click on a target element or at the current mouse position when no element is specified, making it the primary way to trigger double-click interactions in an automation workflow.
When an element is provided, the action moves the mouse to it first to ensure viewport visibility before dispatching the double-click via the WebDriver Actions API.

In RPA it automates file and item opening, inline edit activation, and positional double-click workflows; in automation testing it verifies double-click behavior, inline edit activation, and viewport-scroll interactions.
